hero_sub: Spots are limited. Book your crossing now and secure your place at Portugal's most beautiful nature reserve.
about.p1: Viamar is the historic operator of ferry crossings between the Port of Peniche and Berlenga Island, with over 60 years of experience taking families and adventurers to the most protected nature reserve on the Portuguese coast. On board the Cabo Avelar Pessoa — the largest vessel on the route, with capacity for 180 passengers — the crossing takes 40 to 45 minutes and offers unique views of Portugal's West Coast.
isl_desc: Berlenga is the only inhabited island in the Berlengas archipelago — a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve since 1984. Turquoise waters, dramatic cliffs, sea caves and unique biodiversity found nowhere else in Portugal.
seo.p1: Viamar is the historic operator of ferry crossings between the Port of Peniche and Berlenga Island, with over 60 years of experience taking families and adventurers to the most protected nature reserve on the Portuguese coast. On board the Cabo Avelar Pessoa — the largest vessel on the route, with capacity for 180 passengers — the crossing takes 40 to 45 minutes and offers unique views of Portugal's West Coast.
seo.p2: Berlenga Island has been a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve since 1984, recognised for its exceptional marine biodiversity, dramatic cliffs and Atlantic waters of rare transparency. Covering just 1 km² of land, the island hides isolated beaches, sea caves, the historic Fort of São João Baptista (built in 1509) and wildlife found nowhere else in Europe. A typical visit lasts 4 to 6 hours — enough time to swim, explore the caves and feel the Atlantic isolation that makes Berlenga a truly singular destination.
seo.p3: With a 4.5-star rating across more than 706 verified Google reviews, an IMT licence (Portugal's Mobility & Transport Institute) and over 25,000 passengers carried each year, Viamar is the trusted choice for visiting Berlenga Island. Every ticket includes free cancellation up to 24 hours before departure. The 2026 season opens on 9 May — July and August weekends sell out quickly. Book in advance to secure your spot.
pkg.tag: What do you want to do?
pkg.sub: From a simple crossing to island activities — we have something for every adventurer.

rev.sub: Over 700 verified Google reviews. Written by families, couples and groups who crossed with us.
rev1.text: "An experience that stays with you for life. The crew is super professional and the crossing itself is already an adventure. We arrived at the island completely in love — the caves take your breath away."
rev2.text: "We travelled with 3 small children and it was perfect. Flawless organisation, the boat is safe and comfortable. The kids loved it. We're already planning to come back next summer!"
rev2.name: Ricardo & Family
rev3.text: "We came from Germany specifically to visit Berlenga Island. Viamar made the whole experience seamless — easy booking, punctual, knowledgeable crew. Best day trip in Portugal!"
sched.sub: Summer weekends sell out weeks in advance. Book ahead.
dep.h2: We depart from Peniche, a seaside city
dep.desc: Cais da Ribeira in Peniche is the boarding point for all island crossings. Arrive 20 minutes before departure. Parking is available nearby.
faq.q1: What should I bring for a trip to Berlenga Island?
faq.a1: The essentials: sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses and a water bottle to stay hydrated. Comfortable clothing, a light jacket (preferably waterproof), suitable footwear and a towel. A power bank is also handy since mobile coverage on the island is very weak or non-existent.
faq.q2: Can I cancel or change my booking?
faq.a2: Yes! Please let us know at least 72 hours before boarding. Contact us by phone or email: 911 001 099 | info@viamar.pt.
faq.q3: What can I do on Berlenga Island with my ferry ticket?
faq.a3:
• Hiking trails with stunning views;
  • Visit the historic Fort of São João Baptista;
  • Swim at the crystal-clear beach or have lunch at the island restaurant; snacks at Castelinho with panoramic views;
  • Birdwatching and flora exploration;
  • Optional add-ons: scuba diving, glass-bottom boat cave tour or snorkelling (see the "tickets" page).
faq.q4: Where is the VIAMAR boarding point for Berlenga Island?
faq.a4:
Peniche Marina, next to the Peniche Fortress.

  • Head to the Passenger & Luggage Boarding Zone (as shown on the map);
  • Look for the CHECK-IN flag or a VIAMAR crew member in uniform.

We recommend arriving at least 30 minutes before departure (groups: 1 hour).
faq.q5: Can I buy a ticket on the day of travel?
faq.a5: Yes, subject to availability. In July and August demand is very high — we strongly recommend booking in advance to secure your spot.
faq.a6:
Online: Visa, Mastercard, Apple Pay, MB Way, PayPal, Google Pay, Linkpay or bank transfer (groups only).

At the counter: Visa, Mastercard, Apple Pay, MB Way or cash.

Groups: send proof of payment to info@viamar.pt at least 48h before boarding.
faq.q7: Can I bring luggage on board?
faq.a7:
Yes — backpacks and small bags are welcome. For larger items please contact us in advance: 911 001 099.

Limit: 2 pieces of luggage per person + 1 small carry-on.
faq.q8: How long can I stay on Berlenga Island?
faq.a8: We offer the longest island stay of any ferry operator — up to 5 hours.
faq.q9: Is camping allowed on Berlenga Island?
faq.a9: Wild camping is not permitted. There is an authorised campsite (Parque de Campismo da Berlenga) which requires advance booking. For more information: Peniche Tourism Office.
faq.q10: What are the restrictions in the Berlenga Nature Reserve?
faq.a10:
• Camping only in the designated area with prior reservation;
  • Maximum of 550 simultaneous visitors;
  • Mandatory registration on BerlengasPass and access fee (adults €3; discounts for ages 6–18 and over 65);
  • Stay on marked trails and respect signage;
  • Dispose of waste in the designated bins or take it with you;
  • Do not pick plants or collect geological samples;
  • Do not feed the animals;
  • Loud music is prohibited.
faq.a11: Yes, the vessel has 2 on-board toilets — simple but practical. Our crew ensures they are kept clean and in good condition.
faq.q12: Is it safe to travel with young children or elderly passengers?
faq.a12: Absolutely — our vessel is one of the most popular choices for families with young children and elderly passengers. The boat was designed specifically for the Berlenga crossing with safety and comfort as priorities. Children must always be accompanied by a responsible adult. Passengers requiring special assistance are welcome to let us know in advance.
ck_txt: We use essential cookies to make the site work and analytics cookies to improve your experience. See our privacy policy.

historia.hero_sub:
Over six decades connecting the harbour to the horizon.
A legacy told in crossings, not in years.
ch1_p1: In 1958, someone in Peniche looked towards the horizon and saw Berlenga. Not as an obstacle — as a destination. That is how Viamar was born: from a simple decision, made by the sea, with the island in sight and the boat at the dock.
ch1_p2: It was always a family business. The harbour as office, the sea as road. Bookings were made by word of mouth, trust needed no contract. There was an island to reach — and there were those who knew how to reach it.
fb_quote:
"The sea cannot be explained.
It is crossed."
ch2_p1: Some vessels become part of a place's landscape. The Cabo Avelar Pessoa is one of them — recognised at dawn by generations of Peniche locals who know summer begins there, in the queue at the dock, with the bow pointing towards Berlenga.
ch2_p2: With a capacity of 180 passengers, inspected annually by the National Maritime Authority. But what the numbers do not convey is what the silhouette represents: the promise of a crossing that will happen, as it has happened for decades.
ch3_p1: Berlenga is not easily described. Ancient rocks, water of impossible blue, silence broken by the offshore wind. A protected Nature Reserve, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve — but above all a place that stays in the memory of those who visit.
ch3_p2: Those who go once want to return. It is that return which defines Viamar: the certainty that we will be here, ready to make the crossing, as we have done for over six decades.
arch_sub:
Photographs gathered over decades.
The sea does not change — what changes are the faces that cross it.
cap3: Port of Peniche — years of history
cap4: The island — southern coast of Berlenga
cap8: The route — angles of the crossing
closing_q:
"The sea has no memory.
We keep ours."
ilha.h1:
A world apart,
45 minutes from Peniche
ilha.sub: UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Turquoise waters, dramatic cliffs, sea caves and unique biodiversity found nowhere else in Portugal.
ilha.art.sub: Six moments captured in watercolour — the beach, the arrival, the harbour, the cove, the cave, and the Fort at dusk.

ilha.s1.title: How to reach Peniche
ilha.s1.sub: The gateway to Berlenga is the Marina de Peniche. Once you arrive in Peniche, the Viamar crossing takes you to the island in 45 minutes.
ilha.car.title: By car
ilha.car.body:
From Lisbon: ~1h30 via A8 → IC1 (Peniche exit).
From Porto: ~3h via A1 → A8 → IC1.
From Spain: ~2h30 from Badajoz via A6 → A8.
ilha.car.gps: GPS: Cais da Ribeira, Peniche — Viamar Ferry Berlenga. Parking available nearby.
ilha.bus.title: By bus
ilha.bus.body: Rede Expressos and FlixBus run daily coach services from Lisbon to Peniche, with several departures throughout the day. Journey time: approx. 2 hours.
ilha.bus.note: The Peniche bus stop is ~15 min walk from the boarding marina.
ilha.s2.title: Accommodation & dining
ilha.s2.sub: Berlenga has everything you need for an unforgettable visit — and nothing more. Services are deliberately limited to protect the nature reserve.
ilha.penso.label: Accommodation & Restaurant
ilha.penso.name: Mesa da Ilha
ilha.penso.desc: The island's main accommodation and dining venue. Simple sea-view rooms and a restaurant serving fresh fish and seafood — fall asleep to the sound of the waves and wake up with the island all to yourself. Seasonal opening (June to September).
ilha.penso.note: Bookings via Peniche Town Hall: cm-peniche.pt
ilha.rest.label: Historic Accommodation
ilha.rest.name: Forte de São João Baptista
ilha.rest.desc: The AABerlenga Guesthouse operates inside the historic Forte de São João Baptista, built in the 17th century. A unique experience — sleeping within centuries-old walls surrounded by the Atlantic Ocean. Seasonal opening.
ilha.rest.note: Bookings and info via Peniche Town Hall: cm-peniche.pt
ilha.snack.label: Snack Bar
ilha.snack.name: Castelinho Snack Bar
ilha.snack.desc: Light meals, sandwiches, ice cream, drinks and fresh juices at one of the most photogenic spots on the island — with a direct view of the historic São João Baptista Fortress. The perfect stop mid-exploration.
ilha.snack.note: Outdoor terrace with panoramic views. Seasonal opening.
ilha.camp.label: Camping
ilha.camp.name: Campsite
ilha.camp.desc: The island's only authorised camping area. A minimalist experience under the stars of the nature reserve. It is also possible to rent socalcos — rocky terraces overlooking the sea — for an even more immersive island experience.
ilha.camp.note: Socalco and campsite bookings via the Peniche Tourist Office: cm-peniche.pt
ilha.s3.title: Island code of conduct
ilha.s3.sub: Berlenga is a fragile ecosystem. Protecting it is the responsibility of every visitor — including you.
ilha.s3.intro: Berlenga Island is a Nature Reserve with strictly defined access and behaviour rules. Respecting them is a condition for preserving this unique ecosystem and ensuring visits can continue. Non-compliance may result in exclusion from future trips with Viamar.
ilha.rule1: BerlengasPass required — purchased separately at berlengaspass.icnf.pt (up to €3 per person).
ilha.rule2: No littering — use the designated bins or take your waste with you.
ilha.rule3: Stay on marked trails only — respect all flora protection signage.
ilha.rule4: No wild camping — camping outside the designated area is illegal in the reserve.
ilha.rule5: Max. 550 simultaneous visitors — a limit set by the competent authority to protect the ecosystem.
ilha.rule6: Do not feed the animals — including seagulls and other nesting birds.
ilha.rule7: Respect fauna and flora — do not pick plants or collect geological or mineral samples.
ilha.rule8: No loud music — preserve the natural environment and the well-being of all visitors.
ilha.cta.sub: Limited capacity. Season runs May to September. Book in advance to secure your spot.
t.h1: What you need to know before you travel
t.legal_notice: Official version in Portuguese. Translations are provided for information only — in case of discrepancy, the Portuguese version prevails.
t.s1.title: Cancellations & Refunds
t.s1.p1: Due to adverse weather conditions (e.g. rough seas), mechanical failures or maritime authority orders, cancellations may occur without prior notice. In these situations, the client is entitled to a full refund or trip rescheduling at no additional cost.
t.s1.p2: We advise contacting us at least 48 hours before the scheduled departure; cancellations under these conditions will be refunded or rescheduled. Cancellations made less than 24 hours in advance are non-refundable, except in cases of proven illness or if you have purchased the flexible ticket ("trip protection") — which allows changes up to 30 minutes before departure.
t.s1.p3: Failure to appear at the boarding gate at least 5 minutes before the scheduled departure implies the loss of the ticket and the amount paid, with no right to a refund.
t.s2.title: Schedules & Operational Changes
t.s2.p1: Departure times follow the schedule published in the 'Schedules' section of our website, but may be subject to adjustments or cancellations for operational reasons, such as insufficient passengers or adverse maritime conditions.
t.s2.p2: In such cases, VIAMAR will contact passengers to proceed with a refund or rescheduling of the cancelled trip.
t.s2.p3: The company reserves the right to replace the vessel with one of different capacity, without prior notice, provided that safety and transport conditions are guaranteed.
t.s3.title: Boarding Conditions
t.s3.p1: Passengers must present themselves at the boarding point (Marina de Peniche) at least 30 minutes before departure for check-in and collection of physical tickets. For groups, arrival at least 1 hour in advance is recommended.
t.s3.p2: Minors must be accompanied by a responsible adult or present a duly certified authorisation.
t.s3.p3: Animals are not permitted on board, except guide dogs, with prior notice.
t.s4.title: Safety & Liability
t.s4.p1: Passengers must comply with all crew instructions, particularly those relating to on-board safety, use of life jackets and remaining on the vessel. Non-compliance may lead to refusal of boarding or interruption of the trip, without the right to a refund, whenever justified for safety reasons.
t.s4.p2: Smoking on board is strictly prohibited.
t.s4.p3: Consumption of alcoholic beverages on board is prohibited, as well as boarding by visibly intoxicated passengers; in these situations, boarding may be refused by the crew.
t.s4.p4: During the trip, passengers must remain seated, except for strictly necessary movements, namely to use the restroom.
t.s4.p5: The company is not responsible for loss or damage to personal belongings resulting from exposure to water, improper handling or passenger negligence, without prejudice to any legally provided rights.
t.s4.p6: Passengers with reduced mobility or specific health conditions must inform the company in advance; boarding may be refused if it poses a risk to the safety of the passenger, vessel or other passengers.
t.s5.title: Island Fees — BerlengasPass
t.s5.p1: It is the client's responsibility to acquire the BerlengasPass (via the ICNF platform) and pay the island access fee (up to €3 per person). This fee is not included in the transport ticket price.
t.s5.p2: You can purchase this authorisation directly at berlengaspass.icnf.pt or via the "Get BerlengasPass" button on our website.
t.s6.title: Payment & Reservations
t.s6.p1: Reservations can be made by phone or in person. To secure your booking, we recommend that payment be completed at least 48 hours before the boarding time.
t.s6.p2: Reservations are accepted free of charge, but the trip will only be confirmed after payment, which must be completed no later than 24 hours before departure.
t.s6.p3: If you wish to reschedule your trip less than 24 hours in advance, you must purchase the flexible ticket (trip protection).
t.s7.title: Weather & Sea Conditions
t.s7.p1: The company reserves the right to suspend trips in case of strong winds, port closure by maritime authorities, heavy swell or warnings issued by the Port Authority.
t.s7.p2: In these situations, your trip may be rescheduled at no cost.
t.s7.p3: We recommend that passengers bring adequate seasickness medication if needed. The company provides basic first aid assistance only.
t.s8.title: Behaviour on the Island
t.s8.p1: It is strictly prohibited to litter, camp outside designated areas or disturb the island's fauna and flora. Non-compliance with these rules may result in exclusion from future trips with the company.
t.s9.title: Privacy & Cookies Policy
t.s9.p1: Personal data provided at the time of booking (name, contact details) will be used for operational purposes only and may be shared with authorities if legally required.
t.s9.p2: The company's website uses cookies to optimise your online experience. Clients may adjust cookie settings in their browser preferences.
